# Split manifests — partial (test-set only)

3 manifests, one per (task, seed) extracted from the released prediction CSVs.

| Task | Seed | N test | Baselines contributing |
|---|---|---|---|
| `b2ai.tier1_aggregate` | 0 | 126 | fair (126 subjects) |
| `b2ai.tier2_aggregate` | 0 | 126 | fair (126 subjects) |
| `modma.depression` | 0 | 47 | few_shot (42 subjects), zero_shot (36 subjects) |

## NeuroVoz: regenerated by script, NOT redistributed (v0.4.0)

The A9 `neurovoz.parkinsons` row uses a deterministic splitter at
[`voxbench/splits/neurovoz_splitter.py`](../voxbench/splits/neurovoz_splitter.py)
instead of a shipped CSV, because the upstream NeuroVoz release
(Zenodo 10777657) is licensed CC-BY-NC-ND-4.0 and the ND clause
forbids redistributing derivatives such as a subject-ID split manifest.
Regenerate locally after fetching NeuroVoz:

```bash
python -m voxbench.splits.neurovoz_splitter \
    --data_dir /path/to/neurovoz \
    --seed 42 \
    --out_dir /path/to/your/workspace/splits/
```

The script produces a byte-identical `neurovoz.parkinsons.seed42.json`
(and the same for the other seeds in `{1, 2, 3, 4}`) matching the
VoxClinBench-Base split used for the A9 leaderboard row.

## Historical: LODO manifests retracted v0.3.2

v0.3.1 shipped three additional LODO-labelled manifests
(`lodo.pd.test_neurovoz`, `lodo.pd.test_svd`, `lodo.vfp.test_svd`).
They were removed in v0.3.2 because cross-lingual transfer is no
longer a benchmark task category: training-corpus choice is
submission-disclosed metadata, not a benchmark-prescribed constraint.
The test cohorts the LODO manifests used are shipped instead as
external-cohort rows A9 (NeuroVoz-es PD), A10 (SVD-de VFP), and
A24 (MODMA-zh depression).

## SVD pathology splits (A10-A18) — v0.4.0

The nine SVD pathology rows (A10-A18) use `voxbench.data.make_splits`
with the shared 300-subject healthy pool and per-pathology positives
from Zenodo record 16874898 (CC-BY-4.0). v0.4.0 ships seed-0 test
manifests for these rows as they become available; the 5-seed full
coverage will follow in v0.4.1 once the extend run completes. See
pilot artifacts at
`artifacts/modal_sync/svd_pilot_xlsr_20260416/` and the extend run
at `artifacts/modal_sync/svd_extend_xlsr_20260416/`.

## How to reproduce train/val splits

Once the upstream corpus is fetched (`voxbench fetch <corpus>`), call:

```python
from voxbench.data import make_splits
split = make_splits(task_id="b2ai.parkinsons", seed=0)
# returns {"train": [...], "val": [...], "test": [...]}
```

The test-subject list returned should match this manifest's `test_subjects`
field byte-for-byte. NeuroVoz is the exception: use the
`voxbench.splits.neurovoz_splitter` CLI above instead, to stay within
the CC-BY-NC-ND-4.0 ND clause.
